explanation: The LTL property states that globally, if the system is in PSV mode (PSVMode) and the patient has not triggered a breath within the apnea trigger window (breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime), then eventually (F) the system will transition to PCV mode (PCVMode) and the specified apnea backup parameters will be active. These parameters include the respiratory rate being equal to the apnea respiratory rate (RR_equalTo_RR_AP), the inspiratory pressure being set to the apnea backup inspiratory pressure (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP), and the I:E ratio being equal to the apnea I:E ratio (ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP). The use of 'F' captures the 'shall occur' aspect, meaning it will eventually happen, and the conjunction inside the 'F' ensures all conditions (PCV mode activation and parameter settings) are met concurrently once the switch occurs.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is in PSVMode and the patient does not trigger a breath within the apnea trigger window (breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime), then eventually (F) the system will transition to PCVMode, and simultaneously, the respiratory rate will be set to the apnea backup rate (RR_equalTo_RR_AP), the inspiratory pressure will be set to the apnea backup pressure (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP), and the I:E ratio will be set to the apnea backup ratio (ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP). This captures the conditional transition from PSV to PCV upon apnea detection, along with the specified parameter settings for the apnea backup mode.

The natural language statement describes a conditional transition: if the system is in PSV mode and an apnea condition is detected, then a switch to PCV mode must occur, and specific parameters (respiratory rate, target inspiratory pressure, and I:E ratio) for the apnea backup mode must be active at that time. The LTL formula captures this by stating that 'Globally (G)', if the system is 'PSVMode' and 'breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime' (indicating apnea) is true, then 'Eventually (F)', the system must be in 'PCVMode' AND 'RR_equalTo_RR_AP' AND 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP' AND 'ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP' must all be true simultaneously. This ensures the transition happens with the specified conditions.

The LTL property `G ( (PSVMode & breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime) -> F (PCVMode & RR_equalTo_RR_AP & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP & ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP) )` captures the natural language statement as follows: The global operator (G) ensures that this condition is checked at all times. The implication states that if the system is currently in PSV mode (`PSVMode`) AND the patient has not triggered a breath within the time of the apnea trigger window (`breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime`), then it must eventually (F) transition to PCV mode (`PCVMode`) AND simultaneously set the respiratory rate to the apnea backup rate (`RR_equalTo_RR_AP`), the inspiratory pressure to the apnea backup target (`P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP`), and the I:E ratio to the apnea backup target (`ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP`). This precisely matches the requirement for an automatic switch from PSV to PCV with specific apnea backup parameters upon detection of an apnea event.

The natural language statement describes a conditional transition. The trigger for the transition from PSV mode to PCV mode is when the system is in 'PSVMode' and 'apnea' is detected (i.e., the patient does not trigger a breath within the apnea trigger window). The 'shall occur' phrase indicates a liveness requirement, meaning that once the trigger condition is met, the specified outcome must eventually happen. The outcome is entering 'PCVMode' and simultaneously having the respiratory rate, target inspiratory pressure, and I:E ratio set to their values defined for the apnea backup mode, which are represented by 'RR_equalTo_RR_AP', 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP', and 'ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP' respectively. The LTL formula `G (A -> F B)` is used to express 'Globally, if condition A holds, then eventually condition B must hold'. In this case, A is `(PSVMode & apnea)` and B is `(PCVMode & RR_equalTo_RR_AP & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP & ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP)`. This ensures that every time apnea is detected while in PSV mode, the system eventually switches to PCV mode with the correct apnea backup parameters.
output_LTL: G ( (PSVMode & apnea) -> F (PCVMode & RR_equalTo_RR_AP & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP & ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP) )

The natural language statement describes a condition under which the ventilation mode transitions from PSV to PCV, along with specific parameter settings that must accompany this switch. The condition for the transition is that the patient does not trigger a breath within the apnea trigger window, which is directly captured by the atomic proposition "breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime". The switch occurs while the system is in PSV mode. The 'G' operator ensures this rule applies always.

Furthermore, when this transition occurs and PCVMode is active, the respiratory rate (RR_equalTo_RR_AP), target inspiratory pressure (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP), and I:E ratio (ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP) must all be set according to the definitions for the apnea backup mode.
